objectives: Full architectural review of mx20022 payments runtime workspace
Evaluate service boundaries, dependency graph, trait abstractions, SOLID compliance
important_facts: Clean DAG dependency graph with runtime-core at the leaf
build_participants in app.rs is a 200-line match factory - OCP violation
Engine owns channel construction - tightly coupled to all channel types
Duplicate Outcome enum in mx20022-store and mx20022-runtime-core
Admin has 3 parallel HTTP dispatch mechanisms (host.rs, http.rs, grpc.rs)
Session crate is structurally isolated - no consumer in the workspace
main.rs has 7-way if/else chain for service mode combinations
risks_issues: build_participants factory violates OCP: adding new participant requires modifying app.rs
TX_COUNTER global atomic is not unique across instances
in_flight_transaction_count issues N separate queries instead of one
admin status endpoint loads ALL dead letters and pending expectations to count them

iteration.summary: Completed full architectural review of the mx20022 payments runtime workspace. Read all key source files across 16 crates. Identified clean DAG structure, well-designed trait abstractions for Store/Channel/Participant, proper feature gating. Found critical issues with participant factory violating OCP, duplicate Outcome enums, admin service coupling, and main.rs combinatorial explosion. Session crate appears orphaned.
inputs_consulted: All key source files in runtime, runtime-core, participants, config, channels, store, correlation, session, admin crates

mentions.relevance_note: Code quality findings may overlap with architectural observations
Auth architecture spans channels and admin crates
